risk_of_bias.support_text: "Patients were assigned in a 1:1 ratio to either a galantamine or placebo treatment group, based on a computer-generated randomization schedule prepared at Janssen Research and Development, LCC."
"An Interactive Voice Response System (IVRS) assigned patients to the study treatment..."

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias): All outcomes
risk_of_bias.support_text:
"...the assignment codes were maintained within the IVRS to ensure that investigators, patients, and caregivers were blinded."
"Galantamine was supplied...with matching placebo..."

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of outcome assessment (detection bias): All outcomes
risk_of_bias.support_text: Central allocation and matching placebo ensured blinding of outcome assessors.

risk_of_bias.domain: Incomplete outcome data (attrition bias): All outcomes
risk_of_bias.support_text: Discontinuation rates and reasons for discontinuation were similar between groups (27% for both placebo and galantamine), and results of primary ITT (LOCF) analysis was similar to that of OC analysis. Only one-third of participants randomised completed the study in both groups (placebo 32%, galantamine 33%). Over 40% of participants randomised were terminated early due to interim mortality analyses (placebo 42%, galantamine 40%).
Not all pre-specified outcomes are reported in the published paper, but they are reported at ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT00679627).

characteristics.methods:
Randomised
Double‐blind
Parallel‐group
Placebo‐controlled
Duration: 24 months
characteristics.participants:
Country: 13 European
No. of centres: 127
Diagnosis: probable or possible mild to moderate AD defined by NINCDS‐ADRDA
Inclusion: MMSE 10 to 26; underwent head CT or MRI since diagnosis; with or without cerebrovascular disease; responsible caregiver; written informed consent from patient or appropriate representative
Exclusion: other neurodegenerative or major psychiatric disorders or other causes of dementia; epilepsy, hepatic, renal, or pulmonary disturbances, urinary outflow obstruction, or clinically significant cerebrovascular disease; concurrent medications including memantine and other cognition‐affecting medications or other cholinesterase inhibitors
Total no. of participants: 2051 randomised
Sex: 35.2% male
Age, mean (SD): [placebo 73 (8.7)] [galantamine 73 (8.9)]
characteristics.interventions:
Route: oral
Treatment: extended release galantamine 16 to 24 mg (16 to 24 mg/d)
Treatment commenced at 8 mg daily in the first 4 weeks and increased to 16 mg daily in the second 4 weeks, then further increased to 24 mg daily or remained at 16 mg daily in the final 4 weeks, depending on tolerability.
Control: placebo once daily
characteristics.notes: No. excluded after randomisation: 1384 [No. of participants who did not complete because of early study closure: 830]

sections.text:
Hager K, Baseman AS, Nye JS, Brashear HR, Han J, Sano M, Davis B, Richards HM. Neuropsychiatr Dis Trea. 2014;10:391–401.

On page 393, Figure 1, “Maintenance period (month 6 to 24)” should be “Maintenance period (month 4 to 24)”; “– Gal at stable dose (at least 18 mg/day) as achieved on day 84c” should be “– Gal at stable dose (at least 16 mg/day) as achieved on day 84c”. The correct figure is shown below.

review_title: Galantamine for dementia due to Alzheimer's disease and mild cognitive impairment
